description: Canonical slice registry for the NixOS AI harness. Each slice declares owner paths, runtime surfaces, validation evidence, discoverability surfaces, and governance constraints.

observable.description: Validated plus health and operator-visible signals.

governed.description: Observable plus explicit risk, review, and rollback surfaces.

slices.name: Routing and Orchestration
slices.summary: Coordinator routing, model/profile selection, task classification, and orchestration policy.

invariants: profile names must resolve to declared routing surfaces
intent contracts must be preserved through workflow runs
routing decisions must expose enough information for validation and escalation

notes: This slice is the highest-leverage coordination layer and should default to governed maturity.

slices.name: Switchboard Front Door
slices.summary: OpenAI-compatible proxy that mediates profile-based local and remote model access.

invariants: declared profiles must remain internally consistent
default and continue-local behavior must stay stable for editor workflows

acceptance_signals: profile cards remain aligned with routing callers

notes: Inline Python in Nix remains a design debt called out by the routing architecture review.

slices.name: MCP Server Platform
slices.summary: Shared MCP server stack, tooling exposure, dispatch, and integration patterns across the harness.

invariants: tool contracts must be explicit
shared security middleware should be reused across servers
failure_modes: tool exposure without discoverability or audit

notes: This slice should eventually enforce a more uniform server manifest and middleware contract.

slices.name: AIDB Ingestion and Import Data
slices.summary: Document ingestion, import pipelines, and schema-backed persistence for getting knowledge into AIDB.

invariants: ingested content must avoid secret leakage and preserve provenance
import and persistence paths should remain compatible with declared schema surfaces

acceptance_signals: import routes and schema-backed persistence remain available

notes: This slice should increasingly expose source trust and import validation as first-class acceptance signals.

slices.name: AIDB Retrieval and Search Data
slices.summary: Vector search, RAG retrieval, query validation, and retrieval-serving surfaces for AIDB consumers.

invariants: retrieval surfaces should expose relevant, provenance-preserving results
query validation and retrieval should guard against unsafe or malformed input
failure_modes: retrieval returns empty or stale context

acceptance_signals: vector search and retrieval path remain healthy

notes: This slice should increasingly tie retrieval quality and trust filtering to explicit acceptance checks.

slices.name: Memory and Context Governance Data
slices.summary: Agent memory, temporal facts, layered loading, context memory, and memory-specific governance surfaces.

invariants: stored context should carry enough provenance and validity semantics to be reviewed
memory surfaces should avoid silent drift between repo-owned memory logic and agent-facing tooling
failure_modes: context loading becomes detached from memory governance

acceptance_signals: memory CLI remains queryable and stats-capable
temporal facts and layered loading tests remain healthy

notes: Provenance, trust, retention, and temporal validity should continue becoming stronger explicit fields over time.

slices.name: Workflow Engine
slices.summary: Blueprint-driven planning, persisted runs, phases, acceptance, and workflow execution support.

invariants: workflows must declare phases and exit criteria
runs must preserve intent contracts and reviewer visibility
failure_modes: execution without rollback or acceptance evidence

notes: Workflows are a core control-plane surface and should be treated as governed configuration.

slices.name: AQ Bootstrap and Hints Tooling
slices.summary: Onboarding, task bootstrap, context-card, and hint entrypoints for agents and operators.

invariants: bootstrap tools must remain compact and search-first
hint and context tools must route users to existing harness surfaces instead of hidden internals
failure_modes: context-card guidance diverges from workflow and harness behavior

acceptance_signals: hints and context bootstrap remain available

notes: This slice owns the initial harness entry experience and should remain compact and evidence-first.

slices.name: AQ Runtime Diagnostics Tooling
slices.summary: Runtime diagnosis, remediation planning, and QA phase runners for live service health and incident classification.

invariants: diagnostics should classify the failing layer before proposing changes
runtime actions should preserve reviewer-visible evidence and rollback paths
failure_modes: QA phase drift from runtime planner assumptions

acceptance_signals: runtime planner and diagnose entrypoints run successfully

notes: This slice should stay tightly aligned with live service health and avoid accumulating imperative-only remediation paths.

slices.name: AQ Workflow and Orchestration Tooling
slices.summary: CLI surfaces for workflow execution, orchestration entry, and capability-directed task routing.

invariants: workflow CLI behavior must stay aligned with blueprint contracts
orchestration tools should favor existing harness workflow surfaces over hidden custom flows
failure_modes: workflow CLI diverges from server-side workflow engine
task routing bypasses established workflow blueprints

acceptance_signals: workflow CLI validates and lists workflows
workflow orchestration remains reachable from CLI

notes: This slice should remain a thin operational layer over the workflow engine rather than a separate orchestration system.

slices.name: AQ Reporting and Knowledge Tooling
slices.summary: Reporting, memory lookup, retrieval prewarm, and pattern/gap tooling for operator feedback and knowledge reuse.

invariants: reporting and knowledge tooling should surface actionable operator guidance
memory and prewarm tools should reuse existing harness data stores instead of creating parallel persistence
failure_modes: memory surfaces become detached from AIDB and agent-memory ownership

acceptance_signals: memory CLI remains queryable and stats-capable

notes: This slice should eventually be cross-linked more tightly with the AIDB and memory data slice through shared acceptance signals.

slices.name: Service Health Monitoring
slices.summary: Systemd unit health, HTTP probes, and phase-oriented service checks for the AI stack.

invariants: critical services must have explicit probe coverage
health summaries should distinguish unit failure from HTTP/runtime failure
failure_modes: HTTP probes drift from declared service endpoints

notes: This slice owns the probe and service-status truth used by both operators and runtime tooling.

slices.name: Telemetry and Alerting Observability
slices.summary: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, anomaly detection, profiler, and alert/remediation plumbing.

invariants: telemetry collection should be machine-readable and durable enough for operator review
alert rules should map to actionable remediation or escalation paths
failure_modes: telemetry implementation exists but is not wired into collection surfaces

acceptance_signals: alerting and telemetry config files stay present and parseable

notes: This slice owns the telemetry substrate and alert/remediation policy surface underneath operator dashboards.

slices.name: Readiness and Insights Observability
slices.summary: Repo-native readiness summaries and insight aggregation exposed through dashboard insight routes.

invariants: readiness claims should be backed by probeable code or live endpoint evidence
insight routes should remain consistent with the current harness topology
failure_modes: repo-native readiness logic diverges from live system state

acceptance_signals: dashboard insights remain backed by current repo-native logic

notes: This slice should become the operator-facing home for slice readiness over time.

slices.name: Dashboard Insights and Readiness Surfaces
slices.summary: Read-only operator surfaces for insights, metrics, AI stack health, and readiness reporting.

invariants: operator-facing read-only routes should expose real system state, not only static claims
readiness summaries should remain grounded in repo-native evidence and runtime probes
failure_modes: stale insights presented as current truth
operator readiness endpoints drift from backend evidence sources

acceptance_signals: dashboard insights and health routes return current repo-native readiness data

notes: This slice should eventually expose slice scorecard output directly.

slices.name: Operator Runtime Control Surfaces
slices.summary: Mutating operator surfaces for service control, deployment execution, configuration inventory, and runtime actions.

invariants: mutating operator routes should require explicit, auditable intent
runtime controls should reflect the declared service and deployment topology
failure_modes: dashboard control plane diverges from declared config and live runtime

acceptance_signals: operator control routes stay auditable and runtime-safe

notes: This slice should increasingly bind mutating routes to explicit approval and audit evidence.

slices.name: Workflow and Collaboration Operator Surfaces
slices.summary: Operator-facing workflow automation, multi-agent collaboration, and session-oriented dashboard surfaces.

invariants: workflow surfaces should expose session state that matches actual execution records
collaboration routes should preserve team and plan context across operator-visible flows
failure_modes: workflow UI surfaces drift from workflow execution truth
collaboration state becomes unavailable or inconsistent across sessions

acceptance_signals: workflow and collaboration routes stay consistent with persisted execution and session state

notes: This slice should increasingly tie collaboration/session claims to explicit persisted run evidence.

slices.name: Prompt Registry and Evaluation Guidance
slices.summary: Prompt template registry, prompt evaluation tooling, and scored prompt guidance surfaces.

invariants: prompt templates should remain structured and repo-grounded
prompt score updates should be traceable to explicit evaluation runs
failure_modes: registry entries become stale or unscored

notes: Prompt changes should increasingly require prompt evaluation evidence, not only structural validation.

slices.name: Hints and Context Guidance
slices.summary: Hint generation, context cards, and task-scoped guidance delivery for agents and operators.

invariants: guidance should remain compact, actionable, and repo-grounded
hint and context outputs should drive users toward canonical harness surfaces
failure_modes: context cards diverge from workflow behavior
guidance becomes unreachable from normal harness paths

acceptance_signals: context-card and bootstrap paths stay documented

notes: This slice should stay aligned with workflow and runtime truth rather than accumulating static guidance drift.

slices.name: Agent Instruction Sync and Distribution
slices.summary: Policy distribution, generated agent instruction files, and AIDB import surfaces for agent-facing docs.

invariants: policy sync should not fork across agent instruction surfaces
instruction distribution should preserve source-of-truth ownership
failure_modes: sync script drift from source policy files
AIDB import paths remain undocumented or stale

notes: This slice should increasingly align instruction sync with AIDB import status and explicit retrieval validation.

slices.name: Secrets and Credential Governance
slices.summary: Declarative secret wiring, runtime credential handling, rotation planning, and zero-secrets-in-repo enforcement.

invariants: secrets must not be hardcoded or committed in plaintext
services should consume credentials from secret files or secret-backed env wiring
failure_modes: secret drift between declared inventory and runtime consumers
rotation plans omit affected services or restart groups

acceptance_signals: managed secret inventory stays declarative and rotation-ready

notes: This slice should increasingly tie secret inventory, restart impact, and service credential consumers together.

slices.name: Security Scan and Compliance Automation
slices.summary: Security scanning, vulnerability reporting, compliance checking, and penetration-style automation surfaces.

invariants: security findings should be reproducible from repo-native scripts and stored artifacts
compliance surfaces should reflect current report outputs rather than static claims
failure_modes: scanner outputs become stale or unavailable
compliance routes exist but are not backed by current reports

acceptance_signals: security scans and compliance summaries remain executable and dashboard-visible

notes: This slice should increasingly connect severity thresholds and remediation expectations to explicit acceptance policy.

slices.name: Policy Validation and Governance
slices.summary: Repo policy enforcement, Tier 0 gates, structure linting, tool/policy evaluation, and unattended-sudo governance.

invariants: repo structure policy must be enforced
validation must happen before commit and deploy
declared automation policy should remain machine-evaluable
failure_modes: documented governance checks are not enforced
automation safety assumptions stop matching runtime reality

notes: This is the meta-governance slice that should eventually consume scorecard output as one of its own readiness signals.

slices.name: Operator Security and Audit Controls
slices.summary: Dashboard operator-facing audit, firewall, and runtime security control surfaces.

invariants: operator security controls should remain auditable
firewall mutations should be time-bounded and validated before execution
failure_modes: operator actions are no longer traceable
firewall controls allow unsafe or indefinite mutation

acceptance_signals: audit routes preserve operator evidence and firewall controls remain bounded

notes: This slice should increasingly couple dashboard mutation rights to runtime audit and safety policy evidence.
